name: Topical 5-fluorouracil for skin cancer prevention
summary: A single field course of topical 5-fluorouracil to the face and ears sharply reduced squamous-cell carcinoma requiring surgery in the first year, with no significant effect on basal-cell carcinoma.
description: The VAKCC trial randomized 932 veterans at high risk of keratinocyte carcinoma to a single 2-4 week course of 5-fluorouracil 5% cream versus vehicle, with a median 2.8 years of follow-up.
interpretation: The benefit was large but time-limited: squamous-cell carcinomas requiring surgery fell about 75% in the first year (5/468 vs 20/464), after which the difference faded — implying field therapy clears existing precancerous damage rather than providing lasting protection. Basal-cell carcinoma was not significantly reduced. This squamous-favoring, time-limited pattern is a recurring theme among skin-cancer preventives. See the glossary for risk ratio and NNT.
methodologyNotes: Risk ratios are computed from the reported first-year event counts (2x2): squamous-cell carcinoma requiring surgery 5/468 vs 20/464; basal-cell carcinoma 45/468 vs 50/464. The squamous-cell effect was concentrated in the first year and was no longer significant over the full follow-up. NNT reflects the first year.

studies.author: VAKCC (Weinstock)
studies.citation: Weinstock MA, Thwin SS, Siegel JA, et al. Chemoprevention of basal and squamous cell carcinoma with a single course of fluorouracil, 5%, cream (VAKCC). JAMA Dermatol. 2018;154:167-174.
studies.notes: Squamous-cell carcinoma requiring surgery, first year (~75% reduction). Effect faded after year 1.

studies.author: VAKCC (Weinstock)
studies.citation: Weinstock MA, Thwin SS, Siegel JA, et al. Chemoprevention of basal and squamous cell carcinoma with a single course of fluorouracil, 5%, cream (VAKCC). JAMA Dermatol. 2018;154:167-174.
studies.notes: Basal-cell carcinoma, first year; not significant.
